# Tasks — Cheex String Interpolation Support
*Generated by create_tasks on 2026-03-18*

## Objective
Enable `#{variable}` string interpolation syntax in Cheex template quoted strings, desugaring to existing EEx `<%= variable %>` evaluation at the tokenizer level.

## Scope
- **In Scope**: Recognizing `#{expr}` inside quoted strings and converting to `<%= expr %>` during tokenization; handling escaped `\#{`; nested braces; tokenizer/parser/e2e tests
- **Out of Scope**: Compile-time interpolation optimization, new AST node types, changes to parser/compiler/runtime, interpolation in unquoted strings

## Requirements Traced
| ID | Description | Source | Tasks |
|----|-------------|--------|-------|
| REQ-001 | String variables support `#{expr}` interpolation syntax | ARGUMENTS | 1.1, 1.2 |
| REQ-002 | Existing `<%= %>` syntax continues to work unchanged | Implicit | 1.1, 1.2 |
| REQ-003 | Escaped interpolation `\#{}` produces literal text | Implicit | 1.1, 1.2 |

---

## Architecture Context

### Where This Fits
- Extends the tokenizer's `take_quoted_text/3` function (`lib/juvet/template/tokenizer.ex:590-613`) which collects characters inside quoted strings
- Downstream pipeline (parser, compiler, runtime `eval_map/2`) already handles `<%= expr %>` inside strings — no changes needed there
- The existing escape handling clause at line 606 (`[?\\ | [c | rest]]`) already provides the pattern for `\#{` escape support

### Technical Approach
- Add a new clause to `take_quoted_text/3` that pattern-matches `[?#, ?{ | rest]` and collects until matching `}`
- Convert the collected expression to EEx format: `<%= expr %>` characters prepended to the accumulator
- Leverage the existing escape clause — `\#` already passes through as `\#` via the escaped character handler; we just need the `#{` two-char sequence to trigger interpolation
- No parser/compiler/runtime changes because the output is identical to manually writing `<%= expr %>`

### Key Decisions
- Tokenizer-level desugaring chosen over parser-level AST nodes — simpler, leverages full existing pipeline
- `#{expr}` converts to `<%= expr %>` so that `eval_map/2` handles evaluation at runtime via `EEx.eval_string/2`

---

## Tasks

### Phase 1: Tokenizer Implementation

#### [1.1] Add string interpolation recognition to `take_quoted_text/3`
- [ ] **1.1.1** Add `#{expr}` interpolation clause to `take_quoted_text/3` in `lib/juvet/template/tokenizer.ex`
  - **Produces**: Modified `:text` token with `<%= expr %>` in place of `#{expr}`
  - **Consumed by**: Existing parser `value/1` clause at `parser.ex:330` which strips quotes and passes string through
  - **Replaces**: N/A (new feature)
  - [ ] When `take_quoted_text` encounters `#` followed by `{`, it collects characters until the matching `}` and emits them as `<%= ... %>` in the accumulated string
  - [ ] Handles nested braces correctly (e.g., `#{Map.get(assigns, :name)}` — count brace depth)
  - [ ] Raises a tokenizer error for unclosed interpolation (e.g., `"Hello #{name"`) with line/column info

- [ ] **1.1.2** Ensure escaped interpolation `\#{` produces literal `#{` text
  - **Produces**: `:text` token containing literal `#{` characters (no EEx conversion)
  - **Consumed by**: Same parser path — string treated as static text
  - **Replaces**: N/A
  - [ ] The existing escape clause (`[?\\ | [c | rest]]` at line 606) handles `\#` — verify `\#{expr}` passes through as literal `#{expr}` without triggering interpolation
  - [ ] If the existing clause doesn't fully cover this (since `\#` consumes only `#`, leaving `{` to potentially trigger), add explicit `[?\\, ?#, ?{ | rest]` clause before the interpolation clause

### Phase 2: Testing

#### [1.2] Add tests for string interpolation
- [ ] **1.2.1** Add tokenizer unit tests in `test/juvet/template/tokenizer_test.exs`
  - **Produces**: Test coverage for tokenizer interpolation behavior
  - **Consumed by**: CI/test suite
  - **Replaces**: N/A
  - [ ] Test `"Hello #{name}"` tokenizes to `{:text, "\"Hello <%= name %>\"", {1, 1}}`
  - [ ] Test `"#{greeting} #{name}"` (multiple interpolations) tokenizes correctly
  - [ ] Test `"Hello \\#{name}"` (escaped) tokenizes to literal `#{name}` in the text token
  - [ ] Test `"#{name}"` (interpolation only, no surrounding text) tokenizes correctly
  - [ ] Test unclosed interpolation `"Hello #{name"` raises tokenizer error

- [ ] **1.2.2** Add parser and end-to-end tests
  - **Produces**: Test coverage for full pipeline with interpolation syntax
  - **Consumed by**: CI/test suite
  - **Replaces**: N/A
  - [ ] Test in `parser_test.exs`: `:slack.header{text: "Hello #{name}"}` parses to `%{attributes: %{text: "Hello <%= name %>"}}` — confirming desugaring flows through parser
  - [ ] Test in `template_test.exs`: A template using `"Hello #{name}"` with bindings `[name: "World"]` produces `"Hello World"` in the rendered output
  - [ ] Test in `template_test.exs`: Interpolation inside a for-loop body works (e.g., `text: "#{item}"`)
